title: Define a Derived Component
text: Define a Derived Component Create a derived component by copying a modeled component from the Model tab in a projects's Resource Browser. You must have created a project to contain your application definitions. For more information, see Working with Projects . To define a derived component: Create the derived component: Open the project to which the derived component will belong. In the Resource Browser, click Model and select the modeled component you want to use. Drag and drop it on the project in the Project Structure. Rename the derived component. Double-click the derived component to open it for editing. Note that Modeled Component and Inherits from properties specify the modeled component from which this component is derived. Rename bound entities and fields to the modeled entities and fields you want to use. If you rename a bound object to a modeled entity or field, it will inherit definitions from the modeled object, but it remains bound to the generic entity from which it was copied. Compile the component. During compilation, references in ProcScript to generic entities, fields, and constants are resolved to the correct local values in the derived component. Note: If you need to break the inheritance of a derived component with its modeled component, clear the value of the Modeled Component field. All links to previously inherited properties will be broken and ProcScript modules using generic references will be resolved. You can restore inheritance or switch it to another modeled component by entering the name of the modeled component. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
